verifier_input.doc_snippet:
The proposed research paper provides an overview of an end-to-end implementation of the edge-cloud artificial intelligence of things architecture based on a soft sensor to monitor water quality. In particular, this paper formally presents several relations for virtual sensing, such as COD = f₁(pH, DO, TSS), BOD = f₂(pH, DO, TSS), and NH₃-N = f₃(pH, DO, TSS), which are based on reliable water physics. It also suggests the use of a hybrid ensemble that combines the use of both XGBoost and LSTM neural networks to implement these relationships. The complete five-layer architecture, including physical surrogate sensing, soft sensors, Isolation Forest outlier detection, K-means clustering, Raspberry Pi 4, and Favoriot cloud services, is presented.
